summary_one_line: Input must encode the realm/link-local-all-thread-nodes IPv6 multicast destination (the de ad 00 be ef anchored address bytes) so the equality compare matches.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The IPv6 destination field parsed from the message must equal the link-local or realm-local all-thread-nodes multicast address. In the winning seeds this surfaces as a tightly-concentrated anchor byte run at offsets 0x1a-0x28 (33 00 40 fd de ad 00 be ef 00 ... 01), the exact constant operand the equality compare at line 82 tests against.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winning-resolving seed carries the same fixed byte run at offsets 0x1a-0x28; the BYTE DIFF shows W is perfectly concentrated there (e.g. 0x1c=40(@)x19, 0x1d=fd(.)x19, 0x1e=de(.)x19, 0x1f=ad(.)x19, 0x21=be(.)x19, 0x22=ef(.)x19), meaning all 19 winner samples share that constant, matching the compared address.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds have no such concentration at those offsets — they scatter (e.g. 0x1c shows 00/78/30/c4 +6u, 0x1e shows 5c/00/30/ef +5u tagged DIFFER), so the destination never equals the all-thread-nodes address and the equality compare stays False, blocking the True side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the integer/memory compare at line 82 into the CmpLogObserver buffer, then I2SRandReplace scans the input for one operand and substitutes the other, planting the exact all-thread-nodes address constant into the destination field. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the multi-byte address by blind havoc, which they never do in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is an equality comparison of the parsed IPv6 destination against the realm-local all-thread-nodes address.
The preceding line tests the link-local all-thread-nodes address as the alternative equality operand.
Winning seeds share a fixed anchored byte run at offsets 0x1c-0x22 absent in losers.
The winner anchor run continues with de ad and be ef constants concentrated across all 19 winner samples.
Winning seeds literally contain the address byte run 33 00 40 fd de ad 00 be ef 00 ... 01.
Loser-blocking seeds lack the concentrated anchor and scatter at the destination offsets.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a compared operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive and value_profile losers lack I2SRandReplace.
Both decisive pairs are attributed to the I2S technique delta with high prob_div.
The second decisive pair adds value profile on top of I2S, isolating I2S as the differentiator.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 13380 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 8b0111a41b7191d5` and observed exactly 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 7 of the 8-level ancestor chain, characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the True side on this multi-byte address equality where naive/value_profile resolve the True branch as fast as cmplog/vpc — i.e. removing I2S yields no slowdown — would refute the I2S attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact field offset of the IPv6 destination within the harness input is inferred from byte-diff concentration rather than confirmed against the message-parsing layout, so the anchored run could be partly coincidental seed-sharing.
